title: Microsoft Planner Task
description: Represents a Planner task in Microsoft 365 as defined by the Microsoft Graph API v1.0. A task is contained in a plannerPlan and can be assigned to a plannerBucket within the plan. Each task can be assigned to users, categorized, and tracked for completion.

@odata.etag.description: The ETag of the resource, used for optimistic concurrency control via the If-Match header on PATCH and DELETE operations.

id.description: Read-only unique identifier of the task. It is 28 characters long and case-sensitive. Format validation is performed by the service.

planId.description: The ID of the plan to which the task belongs. Tasks cannot be created without specifying a plan.

bucketId.description: The ID of the bucket to which the task belongs. The bucket must be in the same plan as the task. It is 28 characters long and case-sensitive.

assignments.description: The set of assignees the task is assigned to. Each key is the user ID of the assignee.

appliedCategories.description: The categories (labels) applied to the task. Category names are defined on the plan details.

priority.description: Priority of the task. Valid range is 0-10, with 0 being highest priority. Values 0-1 map to 'urgent', 2-4 to 'important', 5-7 to 'medium', and 8-10 to 'low'. Planner sets 1 for urgent, 3 for important, 5 for medium, and 9 for low.

percentComplete.description: Percentage of task completion. When set to 100, the task is considered completed.

startDateTime.description: Date and time at which the task starts. The Timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time.

dueDateTime.description: Date and time at which the task is due. The Timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time.

completedDateTime.description: Read-only. Date and time at which the percentComplete of the task was set to 100. ISO 8601 format, always in UTC.

completedBy.description: Read-only. Identity of the user that completed the task.

createdDateTime.description: Read-only. Date and time at which the task was created. ISO 8601 format, always in UTC.

createdBy.description: Read-only. Identity of the user that created the task.

hasDescription.description: Read-only. True if the details object of the task has a nonempty description, false otherwise.

conversationThreadId.description: Thread ID of the conversation on the task. This is the ID of the conversation thread object created in the group.

orderHint.description: Hint used to order items of this type in a list view. The format is defined by the Planner order hints specification.

assigneePriority.description: Hint used to order items of this type in a list view when grouped by the user assigned to.

previewType.description: The type of preview that shows up on the task.

activeChecklistItemCount.description: Read-only. Number of checklist items with value set to false, representing incomplete items.

checklistItemCount.description: Read-only. Total number of checklist items present on the task.

referenceCount.description: Read-only. Number of external references that exist on the task.

identitySet.description: A set of identities associated with various events for a resource, such as created by or last modified by.

identity.description: Represents an identity of an actor (user, application, or device).

displayName.description: The display name of the identity.

id.description: The unique identifier of the identity.

plannerAssignments.description: The set of user assignments for a task. Each property name (key) is the user ID of the assignee, and the value is a plannerAssignment object.

plannerAssignment.description: Represents the assignment of a task to a user.

assignedBy.description: The identity of the user that performed the assignment of the task.

assignedDateTime.description: The time at which the task was assigned. ISO 8601 format, always in UTC.

orderHint.description: Hint used to order assignees in a task.

plannerAppliedCategories.description: The categories (labels) applied to a task. Up to 25 categories can be defined per plan. Each property name is a category identifier (category1 through category25) and the value is a boolean indicating whether the category is applied.

plannerTaskDetails.description: Represents the additional information about a task, retrieved via the /details relationship. Includes description, checklist items, and external references.

id.description: Read-only. The unique identifier for the task details. 28 characters long and case-sensitive.

checklist.description: The collection of checklist items on the task.

references.description: The collection of external references on the task.

previewType.description: The type of preview that shows up on the task. When set to automatic, the displayed preview is chosen by the app viewing the task.

plannerChecklistItems.description: A collection of checklist items on a task. Each key is a GUID identifying the checklist item.

plannerChecklistItem.description: Represents a single checklist item on a Planner task.

isChecked.description: Whether the checklist item is checked (completed).

lastModifiedBy.description: Identity of the user that last modified the checklist item.

lastModifiedDateTime.description: The date and time the item was last modified. ISO 8601 format, always in UTC.

orderHint.description: Hint used to order items in the checklist.

plannerExternalReferences.description: A collection of external references on a task. Each key is a URL-encoded URL of the reference.

plannerExternalReference.description: Represents an external reference (link or file) attached to a Planner task.

alias.description: A name alias to describe the reference.

lastModifiedBy.description: Identity of the user that last modified the reference.

lastModifiedDateTime.description: The date and time the reference was last modified. ISO 8601 format, always in UTC.

previewPriority.description: Hint used to set the relative priority order in which the reference will be shown as a preview on the task.

type.description: The type of the reference, used to represent the type of resource (e.g., PowerPoint, Word, Excel, Other).
